title: Energizing Japanese Garlic Stir-fry
description: A dense and energizing Japanese dish featuring garlic, jalapeno peppers, and a touch of sweetness to balance the heat, perfect for diabetic-friendly meals.
instructions: Heat oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and jalapeno, cooking until fragrant and lightly browned.
Add onions and carrots to the pan. Stir-fry for about 5 minutes or until vegetables are slightly softened.
Mix together tahini sauce, brown sugar, and soy sauce in a small bowl. Pour over stir-fried vegetables.
Stir everything together well until the mixture is heated through and caramelized to your liking.
Serve hot as a snack with rice or noodles.
